channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Ghatak UCAV to Feature Indigenous miniaturized Low-Probability-of-Intercept AESA Radar
item.description: India is moving forward with the development of a specialized radar system for its stealth unmanned combat aerial vehicle program, commonly referred to as Ghatak UCAV Program. The new radar is expected to be an advanced Active Electronically Scanned Array (AESA) system designed specifically for stealth operations and equipped with Low Probability of Intercept (LPI) capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL CATS Warrior vs Paninian Swayatt-M1: India’s Loyal Wingman Race Heats Up Between Public and Private Sector
item.description: India’s next generation of unmanned combat aviation is entering a competitive phase as two major Collaborative Combat Aircraft programs begin to take shape. The state-run aerospace giant Hindustan Aeronautics Limited is advancing development of the CATS Warrior, while private sector startup Paninian Aerospace is developing its own Collaborative Combat Aerial Vehicle known as the Swayatt-M1. Both aircraft are designed to operate alongside fighter jets as “loyal wingmen,” but their design philosophies and operational roles differ significantly.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Suryastra Rocket System Cleared Trials Before Induction as Interim Counter to Pakistan’s Fatah-II Rockets
item.description: India’s artillery modernisation has received a major boost with the induction of the Suryastra rocket system, a platform based on the Israeli PULS (Precise and Universal Launching System) architecture. According to sources, the system impressed decision-makers during pre-contract trials by demonstrating exceptional accuracy and operational flexibility, leading to its rapid clearance and induction.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Startup HoverIt’s Divyastra Mk1 Loitering Munition Achieves 5.5-Hour Endurance in Tests, Paving Way for 500 km Precision Deep Strikes
item.description: Lucknow-based defence startup HoverIt has achieved a significant milestone with its Divyastra Mk1, a multi-role autonomous tactical strike system designed as a long-range loitering munition, often referred to as a “killer drone.” The platform recently demonstrated 5.5 hours of flight endurance during key tactical flight tests, enabling it to strike high-value targets at ranges up to 500 km, according to company officials. This performance positions the Divyastra Mk1 as a potent indigenous addition to India’s growing unmanned strike capabilities under the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s S4 Star SSBN to Achieve 90% Indigenous Content, Marking Major Leap in Nuclear Submarine Self-Reliance
item.description: India’s sea-based nuclear deterrent has entered a new phase of maturity with the reported commissioning of INS Aridhaman (S4) on April 3, 2026, and rapid progress on its successor, the S4*—often referred to as “S4 Star.” According to sources, the upcoming fourth SSBN is expected to achieve up to 90 percent indigenous content, the highest level of localization across India’s nuclear submarine program to date. This milestone reflects not just incremental improvement, but a structural shift in India’s ability to design, build, and sustain strategic platforms domestically.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HF-73 Cancellation: The Strategic Mistake That Delayed India’s Indigenous Fighter Jet Capability
item.description: India’s journey toward self-reliance in fighter aircraft development has been long, complex, and often disrupted by policy reversals and shifting strategic priorities. Among the most debated decisions in this trajectory is the cancellation of the HF-73 program—a project that, had it matured, could have positioned India with a viable indigenous combat aircraft decades before the induction of Tejas. The termination of HF-73 did not just halt a single program; it created a capability vacuum that set back India’s aerospace ambitions by years, if not decades.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 800 km BrahMos Set to Change Naval Balance: Pakistan’s Ormara Strategy May No Longer Be Safe
item.description: India’s rapid development of the extended-range BrahMos supersonic cruise missile is reshaping the maritime balance in the Arabian Sea. With trials progressing smoothly and induction targeted for the Indian Navy by 2027-2028, the 800 km variant will deliver a game-changing standoff capability that directly challenges Pakistan’s strategy of dispersing naval assets away from vulnerable forward bases.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MOD issues EoI for indigenous 1000-kg aerial bomb for IAF, Plans to procure 600 of them
item.description: The defence ministry has initiated the process for the indigenous design and development of a 1000-kg aerial bomb akin to Mk-84 for the IAF, aiming to boost India’s ‘Aatmanirbharta’ in the sector, according to senior officials.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: UAV crashes into field during test flight in UP’s Etawah
item.description: An unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) prototype of LAT Aerospace crashed into a field near a village after it lost control during a test flight from the Safai airstrip here, officials said on Saturday. The incident occurred on Friday evening after the company, with prior permission from the Civil Aviation Department, was conducting a test of its UAV prototype at the Saifai airstrip.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Iranians Playing Smart Chess Game’, ‘Trump Living In Fantasy Land’: Former Diplomat K.P. Fabian
item.description: Weighing in on the ongoing West Asia conflict, India’s former Ambassador to Qatar K.P. Fabian on Saturday remarked that the Iranians “are playing a smart chess game” and termed US President Donald Trump’s statements as showing that he is “living in a fantasy land”.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: CDS Anil Chauhan reviews operational readiness along LoC in north Kashmir
item.description: Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) General Anil Chauhan on Saturday reviewed the security and operational preparedness along the Line of Control (LoC) in north Kashmir. Gen. Chauhan visited the strategically-important areas under the Army’s Chinar Corps unit, a defence spokesperson said. During the visit, the CDS reviewed the security landscape and operational posture along the LoC in north Kashmir and commended the formation’s exemplary operational preparedness, doctrinal coherence and resolute professionalism, he added.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Provocative’ statements by Congress about West Asia risking lives of Indians there: PM Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday accused the Congress of making “provocative” statements aimed at straining India’s relations with West Asian nations to politically harm him and thereby, gain political mileage.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Govt hiding reality of foreign surveillance, security at risk: Rahul Gandhi
item.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi on Saturday alleged that the Modi government is putting the security of every Indian at risk by attempting to “cover up its own failures” and concealing the reality of foreign surveillance through Chinese cameras installed at vital locations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ULFA-I militant surrenders before security forces in Arunachal’s Tirap
item.description: A militant of the banned ULFA-I surrendered before security forces in Arunachal Pradesh’s Tirap district, an official said on Saturday. The surrendered militant was identified as self-styled private Jintu Gogoi (23), alias Arun Asom, a resident of Hachara village under Singhadawar Police Station in Assam’s Sivasagar district.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: UP ATS busts suspected Pakistan-linked module, foils planned railway attack
item.description: The Uttar Pradesh Anti-Terrorist Squad (ATS) has arrested four members of a suspected terror module allegedly operating at the behest of Pakistani handlers for “involvement” in planning attacks on key installations, including railway infrastructure, officials said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: admin
